fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Hawaii Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Hawaii - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity hawaii, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to hawaii, pharmacy technician compact hawaii, hawaii pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Hawaii Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: Hawaii Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Care Assurance
board.mailing_address: Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs, PVL Licensing Branch, P. O. Box 3469, Honolulu, HI 96801

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Hawaii.

steps.title: Review Hawaii Pharmacy Technician Requirements
steps.description: Familiarize yourself with the qualifications outlined in Hawaii Administrative Rules (HAR) Chapter 16-95, Section 101.5. Key requirements include being at least 18 years old, possessing a high school diploma/GED, completing a board-approved training program OR holding PTCB/NHA certification, and completing 160 hours of externship (waived if PTCB/NHA certified).

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Go to the Hawaii Professional & Vocational Licensing (PVL) Online Services Portal (https://pvl.ehawaii.gov/pvl/app/landing), register as a new user, and complete the 'Pharmacy Technician' application. Pay the required application fee.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload or mail all required documents as instructed in the online application. This typically includes a passport-sized photograph, proof of high school diploma/GED or enrollment verification, official transcripts/certificate from a board-approved training program OR your current PTCB or NHA certification. If you are licensed in another state, submit verification of your active license.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprint-based Criminal History Check
steps.description: Follow the specific instructions provided during the online application process to complete a fingerprint background check through a board-approved vendor. This is a mandatory step for licensure in Hawaii.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the PVL Online Services Portal for updates on your application status. The Hawaii Board of Pharmacy will review your submission and contact you if any additional information or clarification is needed before issuing your license.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Hawaii offers a temporary or trainee pharmacy technician permit that allows new applicants to begin working in a pharmacy setting under direct pharmacist supervision while completing their certification requirements. Contact the Hawaii Board of Pharmacy for specific temporary permit restrictions and duration.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Hawaii by submitting a new application to the Hawaii Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Hawaii-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a Hawaii pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Hawaii have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Hawaii. To work in Hawaii, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Hawaii?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Hawaii while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Hawaii by submitting a new application to the Hawaii Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Hawaii-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Hawaii
hero.dek: Apply online through Hawaii's Professional & Vocational Licensing (PVL) Online Services Portal (pvl.ehawaii.gov). Create an account, select the Pharmacy Technician application, and submit your details.
